calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-19-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Philip Spalding for Suwalee Thangsumphant (Petitioner).
Amy McEvoy (by telephone) and Morgan Forsey for Chai Thangsumphant.
James Walker for Sunee Thangsumphant (Objector/Surviving Spouse).

The Court stated that it now knows that Chai wants the money that had been previously ordered to him. Mr. Walker stated that the preliminary distribution perhaps was over distribution considering the litigation that has occurred, that if the Decedent's estate is divided by ten heirs it is less than $400,000.00 to each heir and that the Thai estate is much larger than this estate. Mr. Walker also stated that it would be difficult for this Court to order that funds be returned, and suggested that the Court award what ever fees are appropriate, give his Client what ever is left and let any claims go against the Thai estate. Ms. McEvoy stated that her Client takes the position that the $400,000.00 belongs to him and request time to present a brief if the Court is inclined to reduce that amount, and that her Client is okay with the Court ordering appropriate fees and distributing the remainder to Mr. Walker's Client. The Court reminds the parties of their position with respect to the issue of this Court being governed by the Thai Supreme Court's determination. Ms. McEvoy stated that she did not agree that the Thai Supreme Court's determination directly addressed distribution of the assets in this Court and that she is not stating that the issues in the Thai Supreme Court can be re-litigated. Mr. Walker stated that his instructions are to obtain the balance of the estate as quickly as possible for his Client, that his Client does not want to litigate this, that if all the ten children were considered they would have gotten a distribution of $210,000.00 and that instead seven children got $400,000.00. Mr. Spalding confirms that his Client remains neutral on the issue of the $400,000.00 and stated that he has more services to add to his request for extraordinary fees. The Court informed Mr. Spalding that he can not request additional extraordinary fees by way of a declaration and that if this is what he wants he will need to amend his petition. Mr. Spalding stated that he will not present any further request for extraordinary fees and that he stands on what has been submitted. Mr. Walker stated that some of the fees are in response to questions regarding the Thai order, and that he does not feel it is fair for his Client to pay fees for the Thai case and for this case. The Court raised the issue of the statutory fees not being properly calculated. Mr. Spalding stated that he is willing to strike the amount requested for the Paralegal's fees, that the statutory fees included some items that the Examiner noted were not correct, spoke of the loans from the family company, and stated that he is willing to live by the Examiner's suggestion that the loans should not be included in the statutory fee base, but that the Administrator had to account for and use these funds. Mr. Walker requested that the reserve amount of $40,000.00 be eliminated, or reduced to $5,000.00. The Second and Final Account and Report of Administrator, etc. is under submission. The Court stated that it will re-compute the statutory fee base and possibly reduce it, that it will determine the appropriateness of the extraordinary fees with the possibility of reducing that, that the order will include completion of the payment to Chai and order the balance be distributed to the Objector, and that the amount of the reserve will be changed to $5,000.00. The Court also informed the parties that they may have to come in ex parte to obtain an order on the Judicial Council Form to unblock the account.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-08-2019 Continued To Jun-05-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Philip Spalding for Suwalee Thangsumphant (Administrator).
James Walker for Sunee Thangsumphant (Objector).
Amy McEvoy appeared by telephone for Chai Thangsumphant (Child of Decedent).

Mr. Walker stated that his Client feels strongly that she is entitled to the entirety of the estate, that the parties have a difference of opinion on what the Thai decision means, that his Client is relying on the Court's discretion, that his Client has been in litigation for eighteen years and that she wants to bring this to an end. Mr. Spalding stated that his Client is also anxious to have this litigation resolved, that his Client as a Beneficiary has decided not to make any claim to the estate and that he advised his Client that her position should be as a neutral Administrator. Mr. Spalding also stated that his Client is not taking a position to challenge her Mother's position to entitlement to the entire estate as long as the Court takes up the matter of Attorney's fees and Administrator's fees, and allows fees. The Court expressed concern about the rights of the other three Children stating that it is not clear what if any rights they may have, and what notice they have been given. Mr. Spalding stated that they have given notice to the Children's representatives, that they have not responded, that he does not know what they were given or if they received anything, that he believes this is an ancillary estate to the Thai estate and that the Thai decision relates to the entire estate. In addition, Mr. Spalding stated that it had been appealed and that the decision that came down holds that the divorce was invalid, and that the Wife is entitled to half and that the other half goes to all of the ten Children equally. The Court raised the issue of having an equitable responsibility to determine that the other three Heirs are not deprived of their share and noted that there is no petition to determine heirs. Mr. Walker stated that his Client feels that Mr. Spalding and the Administrator should not be awarded fees on her half of the estate. The Court noted that Mr. Walker's Client has no priority over administrative claims. Mr. Spalding provided copies of the bank statements, a declaration regarding retention of the original bank statements and stated that he can provide time records to support his fee requests. Mr. Walker provided copies of his objections. Ms. McEvoy stated that she has had trouble communicating with her Client, that she communicated with her Client's Family and it is her understanding the her Client would like to receive the $400,000.00 check, and requested a continuance to discuss the matter with her Client. Mr. Spalding and Mr. Walker indicated that they are prepared to submit on what has been filed, but Ms. McEvoy is not and once again requested a continuance. The Court will assume that Mr. Spalding's and Mr. Walker's Clients are submitting on the issue of fees being paid prior to any pay out to Mr. Walker's Client. There being no objection by any party, the Court takes judicial notice of the Thai decision without any further attestation. The Second and Final Account and Report of Administrator, etc. is continued to June 5, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation that the request for instructions regarding the returned check will be withdrawn. If not, Ms. McEvoy needs to file something and the petition will need to be amended. Any further pleadings are to be filed no later than May 29th.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
